title: Quiz: QM/MM Coupling
description: Test your understanding of QM/MM methods

questions.question: What is the main purpose of QM/MM methods?

options.text: To speed up pure QM calculations
To combine quantum accuracy for reactive regions with classical efficiency for the environment
To eliminate the need for force fields

questions.explanation: QM/MM combines quantum mechanical treatment of small, chemically important regions (like enzyme active sites) with fast molecular mechanics for the large surrounding environment, enabling study of reactions in realistic environments.

questions.question: What is 'electrostatic embedding' in QM/MM?

options.text: Including MM point charges in the QM Hamiltonian

questions.explanation: Electrostatic embedding includes MM point charges as an external potential in the QM calculation, allowing the MM environment to polarize the QM electron density for more accurate electronic structure.

questions.question: What is a 'link atom' in QM/MM?

options.text: A capping atom (usually H) added to satisfy valence at QM/MM boundaries
A special force field atom type
The central atom of the QM region

questions.explanation: Link atoms (usually hydrogen) are added at QM/MM boundaries where covalent bonds are cut. They cap the dangling valences of QM atoms and are constrained to lie along the original bond direction.

questions.question: QM/MM boundaries should preferably be placed across polar bonds like C-N or C-O.
questions.explanation: False. QM/MM boundaries should be placed across non-polar bonds (like C-C) when possible. Cutting polar bonds introduces larger errors due to the different electronic properties on each side of the cut.

questions.question: For enzyme QM/MM simulations, the QM region typically includes:

options.text: The active site, substrate, and key catalytic residues

questions.explanation: The QM region should include atoms directly involved in the chemical reaction: the substrate, catalytic residues, and any cofactors. This typically means 50-200 atoms for enzyme catalysis studies.

questions.question: Which are valid components of the QM/MM energy? (Select all)

options.text: Pure QM energy of the QM region
Pure MM energy of the MM region

questions.explanation: The QM/MM energy consists of E_QM (quantum energy of QM atoms), E_MM (classical energy of MM atoms), and E_QM/MM (interaction between QM and MM regions). Relativistic corrections may be included in the QM part but are not a separate QM/MM component.
